senses.definition: a building where Christians go to worship and practice their religion

examples.x: They attended Sunday service at the local church with their family.
She lit a candle in the church to offer a prayer for her loved ones.
They celebrated Easter at the church, singing hymns and participating in the religious ceremony.
The stained glass windows in the church depicted scenes from the Bible.
He volunteered at the church's soup kitchen to help feed the homeless.

senses.definition: a small structure with closed sides in which people can make a phone, vote, etc. in private
a large, imposing building, especially one that is impressive in size or appearance

examples.x: The ancient cathedral stood as a majestic edifice in the center of the city.
The government edifice housed offices for various departments.
The library was an impressive edifice of knowledge and learning.
They admired the historic edifice of the castle perched on the hilltop.
The ancient edifice stood tall amidst the modern city skyline.

senses.definition: a huge wall built to keep water from entering an area or to contain and use it as a power source to produce electricity

examples.x: The dam controls the river’s water level.
Engineers designed the dam to generate hydroelectric power.
The town relies on the dam for its water supply.
The construction of the dam displaced several villages.
Heavy rains put pressure on the dam’s structure.

senses.definition: a structure that is designed to be easily transported or moved from one location to another, providing temporary or mobile accommodation, workspace, or storage solutions

examples.x: The company set up a portable building on the construction site to use as an office.
For the event, they rented a portable building to house registration and information booths.
A portable building was used as a temporary classroom while the school underwent renovations.
They placed a portable building near the parking lot to serve as a security office.
After the fire, the business used a portable building as a temporary shop until the main building was rebuilt.

senses.definition: the largest and most important church of a specific area, which is controlled by a bishop

examples.x: The cathedral is known for its stunning Gothic architecture and intricate stained glass windows.
Every Sunday, the community gathers at the cathedral for a traditional worship service.
Tourists flock to the cathedral to admire its historical significance and breathtaking design.
The cathedral's bell tower can be seen from miles away, making it a landmark in the city.
During the holiday season, the cathedral is beautifully decorated with lights and festive ornaments.

senses.definition: a very large, often roofless, structure where sports events, etc. are held for an audience

examples.x: The new stadium was packed with fans, all eagerly awaiting the kickoff of the championship football game.
Concerts and major sporting events often draw huge crowds to the stadium, creating an electric atmosphere.
The city invested in upgrading the stadium to include modern amenities, such as improved seating and high-definition video screens.
As the sun set, the stadium lights illuminated the field, signaling the start of the evening's baseball game.
The stadium's design allows for excellent acoustics, making it a popular choice for both sports events and live music performances.

senses.definition: a large, tall building with long blades, called sails, that uses wind power to make flour out of grain or pump water

examples.x: The old windmill was restored as a historical site.
Traditional windmills were used to grind wheat into flour.
The windmill’s blades turned rapidly in the strong breeze.
The farm installed a modern windmill to generate electricity.
Many windmills in the Netherlands have been preserved as landmarks.

senses.definition: a small, separate building located outside of a main residence, traditionally used as a toilet or restroom facility, typically containing a simple pit or composting system
a simple and small cottage-like building that is built to store things or shelter animals

examples.x: The farmer built a wooden shed to provide shelter for his goats during the winter.
The children turned the old shed into a cozy clubhouse for their games.
During the storm, the family stored their bicycles in the backyard shed to keep them dry.
She bought a new shed to organize her gardening equipment and supplies.

senses.definition: a tall, cylindrical structure typically used for storing and preserving bulk quantities of grain, seeds, or other agricultural products, often found on farms or in agricultural settings
a temporary structure, such as a tent or stand, in which exhibitions and public events are held
a structure built over a river, road, etc. that enables people or vehicles to go from one side to the other

examples.x: They crossed the bridge to reach the other side of the river.
She paused on the bridge to take photographs of the city skyline.
They drove across the bridge to visit the neighboring town.
The old stone bridge was a historic landmark in the region.

senses.definition: a modern building that is very tall, often built in a city

examples.x: The city skyline is dominated by a new skyscraper that towers over all the other buildings.
He works in a skyscraper located in the financial district.
The architect designed a futuristic skyscraper with glass facades.
From the top of the skyscraper, you can see the entire city.
The skyscraper was built to withstand high winds and earthquakes.

senses.definition: a tall and often narrow building that stands alone or is part of a castle, church, or other larger buildings

examples.x: A tall tower stood at the center of the medieval castle.
The tower was built to support a large clock.
A new skyscraper will have a tower at its peak for decoration.
The tower collapsed during the storm due to strong winds.

senses.definition: a large, tall, and round building that cools down water for industrial purposes

examples.x: The power plant relies on a cooling tower to regulate the temperature of its machinery.
Cooling towers are essential in large factories where machines generate significant heat.
The engineers checked the cooling tower to ensure it was functioning properly during the summer heat.
As the water in the cooling tower evaporates, it helps cool down the plant's equipment.
The maintenance team performed routine checks on the cooling tower to prevent overheating in the factory.

senses.definition: a small store with an open front selling newspapers, etc.

examples.x: She bought a magazine from the kiosk at the train station before boarding her train.
The mall installed interactive kiosks to provide shoppers with information about store locations and promotions.
The food court has a variety of kiosks offering different types of cuisine, from Mexican to Asian.
He rented a space at the local fair to set up a kiosk selling handmade jewelry.
The airline introduced self-service check-in kiosks at the airport to streamline the boarding process.

senses.definition: a structure built in honor of a public figure or a special event

examples.x: The towering monument was erected in honor of the soldiers who fought bravely in the war.
Tourists flock to the city to see the historic monument that commemorates a significant battle.
The local community raised funds to build a monument dedicated to the town’s founder.
The monument features intricate carvings that tell the story of the event it represents.
Every year, a memorial service is held at the monument to remember those who lost their lives.

senses.definition: a building in which sea creatures, such as fish, sharks, etc., are kept and displayed for the public

examples.x: They watched a dolphin show at the aquarium.
The aquarium is a popular attraction for families and tourists alike.
The aquarium’s largest tank holds over a million liters of water.
The children were excited to visit the aquarium to see the sharks.
She spent hours observing jellyfish at the aquarium.

senses.definition: a glass structure used for growing plants in and protecting them from cold weather

examples.x: They built a greenhouse to grow vegetables year-round.
The greenhouse was filled with tropical plants and flowers.
She spent the morning watering the plants in the greenhouse.
They installed a ventilation system in the greenhouse to regulate temperature.
The school’s greenhouse is used to teach students about botany.

senses.definition: a structure or building with niches or compartments designed to hold the cremated remains of deceased individuals, serving as a final resting place or memorial
a grand above-ground structure used for entombing the deceased or as a memorial

examples.x: The family visited the mausoleum to pay their respects to their ancestors.
The grand mausoleum stood in the center of the cemetery, a testament to the family's legacy.
After the funeral, the casket was placed inside the mausoleum for permanent resting.
The mausoleum was designed with intricate carvings to honor the memory of the famous historical figure.

senses.definition: a public monument built to honor the people who died in a war and are buried elsewhere

examples.x: The town built a cenotaph in the square to honor the soldiers who died in the war.
People gathered around the cenotaph every year to pay their respects on Memorial Day.
A beautiful wreath was placed at the base of the cenotaph to mark the anniversary of the tragedy.
The mayor gave a speech in front of the cenotaph, praising the bravery of those who had sacrificed their lives.
A quiet moment of silence was observed near the cenotaph during the remembrance ceremony.

senses.definition: a tall column made of stone with four sides and a pyramid-like top, used as a monument to honor an important event or  person
an overground or underground grave that is large in size and is often made of stone

examples.x: They placed flowers at the entrance of the tomb to honor their loved one.
Historians studied the artifacts found in the tomb for clues about the past.
Visitors came from far and wide to see the famous royal tomb.
The tomb was sealed to protect the remains inside from damage.

senses.definition: a building used for worshiping one or several gods, used by some religious communities, especially Buddhists and Hindus

examples.x: They visited the ancient temple to offer prayers and seek blessings.
The temple complex included shrines dedicated to different deities.
She performed rituals at the temple during the festival of Diwali.
They meditated in the serene atmosphere of the Buddhist temple.
He made a pilgrimage to the temple to fulfill a vow made to the deity.

senses.definition: a group of buildings that are related or designed to function together, typically sharing common features or purpose

examples.x: The new building complex includes both residential apartments and office spaces.
The shopping mall is part of a larger building complex that also houses a hotel and a gym.
The university's campus is made up of several building complexes spread across the city.
The residential building complex has shared amenities like a swimming pool and playground.

senses.definition: a small building with a high ceiling that is primarily used to provide a covered space for coaches and carriages when they are not in use

examples.x: The old carriage house was converted into a cozy guest cottage for visiting family and friends.
The family restored the carriage house to its original design, keeping the large doors and wooden beams.
The estate included a carriage house that was used to store vintage cars and other equipment.
They turned the carriage house into a studio, using the spacious area for painting and crafting.
The carriage house still had the tracks on the floor where the horses' carriages used to be stored.

senses.definition: a structure or shelter specifically designed for storing firewood or logs

examples.x: She spent the afternoon organizing the firewood in the woodshed, preparing for the cold season.
The smell of fresh pine filled the air as we unloaded the wood into the woodshed.

senses.definition: a building on a farm in which people keep their animals, straw, hay, or grains
a passage dug through or under a mountain or a structure, typically for cars, trains, people, etc.

examples.x: The train disappeared into the tunnel with a roar.
We drove through a dark tunnel carved into the mountainside.
Workers are repairing the tunnel to improve traffic flow.
The city constructed a new tunnel to ease congestion on the roads.
The subway system includes several tunnels that connect different parts of the city.

senses.definition: a small outdoor structure or building used for storing and organizing tools, equipment, and other items typically used for gardening, maintenance, or DIY projects
a heated building that is made of glass and is used for growing in plants that need warm weather

examples.x: The hothouse was filled with exotic flowers and tropical plants that could not survive outside.
They spent the winter months in the hothouse, growing vegetables that needed extra warmth.
He built a hothouse in the backyard to grow his favorite tropical fruits, even in the colder months.
The gardener tended to the plants in the hothouse, ensuring the temperature remained steady.
In the hothouse, the tropical plants flourished, with lush greenery and vibrant blooms year-round.

senses.definition: a large, singular stone block, frequently used as a pillar or memorial

examples.x: The ancient monolith stood tall in the desert, a mysterious symbol of an ancient civilization.
They discovered a monolith while hiking through the mountains, its smooth surface contrasting with the rough terrain.
The park was famous for its giant stone monolith, which attracted tourists from all over the world.
The artist created a modern monolith from stainless steel, which became the centerpiece of the gallery.
Some believe the monolith in the forest was placed there by early settlers as a marker for travelers.

senses.definition: a decorative building of no practical use that was built in the past, often in a large garden belonging to a country house
